item.title: SpeechRecognitionManager: no deinit cleanup — audio engine leaks if deallocated while recording
item.intent: Audio resources must be released on dealloc — a live mic after the editor closes is a privacy violation and a resource bug
item.description: SpeechRecognitionManager has no deinit. If the instance is deallocated while state is .recording or .starting, AVAudioEngine and SFSpeechRecognitionTask are never stopped or cancelled. The microphone tap remains active and the OS audio session is not released. stopRecording() exists but is not called on dealloc.
steps: In Sources/EMEditor/SpeechRecognitionManager.swift, add deinit: if audioEngine \!= nil { audioEngine?.stop(); audioEngine?.inputNode.removeTap(onBus: 0); audioEngine = nil; recognitionTask?.cancel(); recognitionTask = nil; state = .idle }
Verify the deinit executes on the main actor (SpeechRecognitionManager is @MainActor — Swift guarantees deinit runs on the actor's executor for actor-isolated types)

Test: in EMAITests or EMEditorTests, create SpeechRecognitionManager, set audioEngine to a non-nil AVAudioEngine stub, then nil the manager reference and verify via weak var that the instance deallocates and audioEngine cleanup path was hit (use a spy/subclass or test hook)
acceptanceCriteria: Deallocating SpeechRecognitionManager while audioEngine is non-nil stops the engine and removes the tap
Deallocating SpeechRecognitionManager while recognitionTask is non-nil cancels the task
Unit test passes verifying dealloc path cleans up audio resources
